disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Vomiting occurs within a minute or two of every milk (breast) feed in a 6-day-old
trigger_question_examples: When does he vomit — is it linked to feeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He brings the milk straight back up within a minute or two of every breastfeed — it happens every single time he nurses.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapidly progressive jaundice appearing overnight and spreading down the body over 24 hours
trigger_question_examples: When did the yellow colour start and is it spreading?
Where on his body is the yellow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The yellow came up overnight on his face and eyes, and now it's spread down to his tummy — it's got much more yellow really quickly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New profound lethargy — floppy, difficult to rouse, weak cry — developing over the last few hours
trigger_question_examples: How alert is he — can you wake him easily?
Is he floppy or is his cry weak?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone really floppy and I can hardly wake him — his cry is weak and he just wants to sleep. It's much worse in the last few hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Brief jittery/shaky episode after the last feed (hypoglycaemia proxy)
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any shaking, jitteriness or unusual movements?
Did anything happen right after a feed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — right after his last feed he went all jittery and shaky for a little while, then went floppy again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor feeding with weight loss since birth (birth weight ~3.3 kg, now appears to have lost weight)
trigger_question_examples: How has his feeding and weight been since birth?
Has he been putting on weight or losing it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was 3.3 kilos at birth but he's feeding badly and looks like he's lost weight — his clothes are loose on him now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tiny red-purple spots on the chest that do not fade
trigger_question_examples: Are there any rashes or spots on his skin?
Do the spots disappear if you press on them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There are some tiny red-purple spots on his chest that have just come up — they don't seem to fade when I press on them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He feels cold to touch and looks pale/mottled
trigger_question_examples: Does he feel warm or cold?
What does his skin colour and warmth look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He feels cold to touch and looks a bit pale and blotchy, not his usual pink self.
